quiz.title: Unit 6 Quiz 1, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Introduction to Chemical Reactions
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Chemical Reactions, Signs of Chemical Reactions, Synthesis, Decomposition, Single Replacement, Double Replacement, Combustion, Exothermic vs Endothermic, Law of Conservation of Mass

questions.question: Which of the following is a chemical reaction?
questions.explanation: Rust forming on iron is a chemical reaction (oxidation) that produces a new substance (iron oxide). Melting, dissolving, and evaporation are physical changes that do not create new substances.

questions.question: The general form of a synthesis reaction is:
questions.explanation: A synthesis reaction (also called a combination reaction) occurs when two or more reactants combine to form a single product: A + B → AB.

questions.question: In a decomposition reaction:
options: Two or more substances combine to form one compound
A compound breaks down into simpler substances
questions.explanation: A decomposition reaction occurs when a single compound breaks down into two or more simpler substances: AB → A + B.

questions.question: What type of reaction is represented by: $2\text{KClO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{KCl} + 3\text{O}_2$?
questions.explanation: This is a decomposition reaction because a single compound (potassium chlorate) breaks down into two simpler substances (potassium chloride and oxygen gas).

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a single replacement reaction?
questions.explanation: In a single replacement reaction, one element replaces another in a compound. Here, zinc replaces hydrogen in hydrochloric acid to form zinc chloride and hydrogen gas.

questions.question: Which type of reaction is represented by: $\text{NaOH} + \text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{NaCl} + \text{H}_2\text{O}$?
questions.explanation: This is a double replacement reaction (also called double displacement). The cations and anions exchange partners: Na⁺ combines with Cl⁻, and H⁺ combines with OH⁻ to form water.

questions.question: The reaction $2\text{Mg} + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{MgO}$ is an example of:
questions.explanation: This is a synthesis reaction where two reactants (magnesium and oxygen) combine to form a single product (magnesium oxide). It could also be considered a combustion reaction, but synthesis is the most specific classification.

questions.question: In a combustion reaction, the reactant is usually:
questions.explanation: Combustion reactions typically involve a hydrocarbon (compound containing carbon and hydrogen) reacting with oxygen gas ($\text{O}_2$) to produce carbon dioxide and water.

questions.question: Which of the following is a product of a combustion reaction?
questions.explanation: Complete combustion of a hydrocarbon produces carbon dioxide ($\text{CO}_2$) and water ($\text{H}_2\text{O}$). Incomplete combustion may also produce carbon monoxide or carbon.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a sign of a chemical reaction?
questions.explanation: Obtaining kerosene from crude oil is a physical separation process (fractional distillation), not a chemical reaction. Formation of a precipitate, color change, and temperature change are all indicators that a chemical reaction may have occurred.

questions.question: Which law must be obeyed when balancing chemical equations?
questions.explanation: The Law of Conservation of Mass states that matter cannot be created or destroyed in a chemical reaction. Therefore, chemical equations must be balanced so that the number of atoms of each element is the same on both sides.

questions.question: When iron rusts, the reaction is:
questions.explanation: Rusting is an exothermic reaction, meaning it releases heat to the surroundings. However, the reaction is slow, so the heat release is not readily noticeable.

questions.question: Which type of reaction is this? $\text{C}_3\text{H}_8 + 5\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 3\text{CO}_2 + 4\text{H}_2\text{O}$?
questions.explanation: This is a combustion reaction. Propane ($\text{C}_3\text{H}_8$), a hydrocarbon, reacts with oxygen to produce carbon dioxide and water, releasing heat and light.

questions.question: Which is an example of a double replacement reaction?
questions.explanation: This is a double replacement reaction where the cations and anions exchange partners: Ag⁺ combines with Cl⁻ to form AgCl (precipitate), and Na⁺ combines with NO₃⁻ to form NaNO₃.

questions.question: A reaction that absorbs energy from the surroundings is called:
questions.explanation: An endothermic reaction absorbs energy from the surroundings, often in the form of heat. This results in a decrease in temperature of the surroundings. Exothermic reactions release energy.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the basics of chemical reactions! 🧪⚡
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the different reaction types and their characteristics.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on identifying synthesis, decomposition, and replacement reactions.
scoreMessages.0: Please review chemical reaction types, signs of chemical reactions, and energy changes.
